searchDocs.title: ShatterSizeConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface ShatterSizeConfig { Effects: {[string]: any}?-- same shape as ShatterConfig.Effects. Size: number?-- overrides the controller's own sizing rule for just this re-shatter. }

searchDocs.title: Define​
searchDocs.content: </> Facet.Define(config: DefineConfig) → VoxelController Creates a VoxelController. Type picks Relative (dynamic sizing via RecommendedVoxelSize/MinimumVoxelSize, multithreaded, swappable Drivers) or Absolute (fixed VoxelSize). Pass Host to scope every Shatter call on the controller to one instance without naming a Target each time.

searchDocs.title: HitboxConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface HitboxConfig { Size: Vector3? -- box/region extents, in studs, centered on CFrame. Ignored by Raycast. CFrame: CFrame? -- where the hitbox sits in the world. Defaults to CFrame.new() (the origin) if omitted. ViewVector: Vector3? -- cast direction for QueryType.Raycast only; ignored by every other QueryType. Color: Color3? -- cosmetic only - tints a visualized hitbox if you render one yourself. Facet never draws it for you. QueryType: Enums.QueryType?-- Box (default) | Sphere | Region | Raycast. Picks which exact-shape test runs after the octree broad phase. HitboxType: Enums.HitboxType?-- Partial (default, voxelizes whatever's detected) | Whole (returns fully-contained parts untouched, no slicing). Shape: Enum.PartType?-- reserved for a future visualized-hitbox helper; not read by Shatter today. Copy: Instance? -- reserved for a future "clone the hitbox as a real part" helper; not read by Shatter today. }

searchDocs.title: DefineConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface DefineConfig { Type: Enums.ControllerType-- Relative (dynamic sizing, requires RecommendedVoxelSize) or Absolute (fixed VoxelSize). Required. RuntimeType: Enums.RuntimeType?-- Instant (default, runs every Shatter immediately) or Timeline (queues calls until :Start()). Host: Instance? -- scopes every Shatter call on this controller to one instance without naming a Target each time. MinimumVoxelSize: number?-- Relative only. Floor Shatter will shrink voxels to under load. Defaults to RecommendedVoxelSize (no shrink) if omitted. RecommendedVoxelSize: number?-- Relative only. Target voxel size the controller sizes toward. Required for Relative. Drivers: {VoxelizationMethod: string?}?-- reserved for swapping the slicing backend; not read by Shatter today. VoxelSize: number?-- Absolute only. The fixed cube size every voxel is sliced to. Required for Absolute. }

searchDocs.title: Shatter​
searchDocs.content: </> VoxelController:Shatter( a: Instance | ShatterConfig,-- a Target when the controller has no Host, otherwise the ShatterConfig directly. b: ShatterConfig?-- the config, when a was a Target. ) → PendingResult-- chain off it with :Next(Enums.Event.X):Then(callback).

interface ShatterConfig { Hitbox: HitboxConfig?-- scopes detection to a region of the target. Omit entirely to voxelize the whole target. Effects: {[string]: any}?-- keyed by Enums.Effects (Explode = { Radius: number }, Anchored = boolean), or the string key "Velocity" for a raw Vector3. Cleanup: (Enums.Cleanup | (Voxel) → ())?-- Rebuild | Fade | Destroy, or your own function(voxel) ... end. Only ever runs against Result.Voxels.Partial. CleanupDuration: number?-- seconds the Cleanup tween takes (Fade/Rebuild only - Destroy is instant). Defaults to 0.5 for Fade, 0.6 for Rebuild. }  Shatter(Config) when the controller has a [Host], Target is implied. Shatter(Target, Config) otherwise. Hitbox is optional in either form - omit it to shatter the whole Target/Host directly with no detection step.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> VoxelController:Start() → () Flushes the Timeline queue in call order. No-op for Instant controllers - they never queue, every :Shatter() call already ran by the time it returns.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> VoxelController:Destroy() → () Clears any queued Timeline jobs that haven't run yet. Doesn't touch parts already produced by past :Shatter() calls - those live on independently under workspace._Bin.Voxels.

searchDocs.title: Result​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Result { Voxels: { Whole: {Voxel}, Partial: {Voxel}, Extra: {Voxel} }-- every produced Voxel, split three ways. Whole: isolated but never sliced (HitboxType.Whole only). Partial: the actual debris - sliced, and the only bucket Cleanup ever runs against. Extra: produced but outside the hitbox - greedy-meshed into a handful of boxes and left alone unless you Cleanup it yourself. Instance: Instance? -- the Target/Host this Shatter call ran against. CollisionPoint: Vector3? -- reserved for a future raycast-driven Shatter helper; not populated today. SurfaceNormal: Vector3? -- reserved for a future raycast-driven Shatter helper; not populated today. }

searchDocs.title: ControllerType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.ControllerType: { Relative: "Relative", Absolute: "Absolute" } Passed as DefineConfig.Type. Relative sizes dynamically toward RecommendedVoxelSize and splits multi-part Shatter calls across the Workers Actor pool; Absolute uses a fixed VoxelSize and never threads.

searchDocs.title: RuntimeType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.RuntimeType: { Instant: "Instant", Timeline: "Timeline" } Passed as DefineConfig.RuntimeType. Instant (default) runs every Shatter call the moment it's called; Timeline queues calls until Controller:Start() flushes them in order.

searchDocs.title: HitboxType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.HitboxType: { Whole: "Whole", Partial: "Partial" } Passed as HitboxConfig.HitboxType. Partial (default) slices whatever the hitbox finds; Whole returns parts the hitbox fully contains untouched, with no slicing.

searchDocs.title: QueryType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.QueryType: { Raycast: "Raycast", Box: "Box", Sphere: "Sphere", Region: "Region" } Passed as HitboxConfig.QueryType. Mirrors Roblox's own spatial query kinds. Box/Region run through the Octree + an exact box/AABB test, Sphere through the Octree + a distance test, and Raycast casts directly through the engine (single closest hit - see the README's Raycast caveat).

searchDocs.title: Effects​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.Effects: { Explode: "Explode", Anchored: "Anchored" } Keys into ShatterConfig.Effects. Explode = {Radius: number} pushes the voxel outward from the Shatter origin; Anchored = boolean sets the produced part's Anchored directly.

searchDocs.title: Cleanup​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.Cleanup: { Rebuild: "Rebuild", Fade: "Fade", Destroy: "Destroy" } Passed as ShatterConfig.Cleanup, or to Voxel:Cleanup directly. Destroy is instant; Fade tweens Transparency to 1 then destroys; Rebuild tweens the voxel's CFrame back to OriginalCFrame and never destroys it.

searchDocs.title: Event​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.Event: { NewVoxel: "NewVoxel", Destroyed: "Destroyed" } Passed to PendingResult:Next. NewVoxel fires once, right after voxelization/Effects; Destroyed fires once every Partial voxel from that call has actually been destroyed by Cleanup (never fires if Cleanup was Rebuild or unset).

searchDocs.title: computeAsync​
searchDocs.content: </> Workers.computeAsync(jobs: {Job}) → {{Voxelize.MergedVoxel}}

interface Job { Grid: Grid-- a part's already-built grid (Voxelize.buildGrid) - plain data, no Instance reference. Hitbox: PointHitbox?-- the classification hitbox, in the same Instance-free shape Voxelize.classifyPoint expects. nil means "no hitbox" - every cell classifies Partial. }  Runs Voxelize.computeCells() for every job in jobs, spread across the Actor pool, and returns the results in the same order jobs was given. Falls back to running every job serially on the calling thread (still correct, just not parallel) if the Actor pool isn't available.

searchDocs.title: Strategies​
searchDocs.content: Enums.Cleanup.Destroy - instant. Ignores CleanupDuration.Enums.Cleanup.Fade - tweens Transparency to 1 over CleanupDuration seconds (default 0.5), then destroys the part.Enums.Cleanup.Rebuild - anchors the voxel and tweens its CFrame back toOriginalCFrame over CleanupDuration seconds (default 0.6), reassembling the target in place instead of destroying anything.a function - Cleanup = function(voxel) ... end runs your own logic against eachPartial voxel instead. voxel is a full Voxel, so you still getvoxel.Instance, voxel.OriginalCFrame, and voxel.Kind.

searchDocs.title: Per-voxel cleanup​
searchDocs.content: Every Voxel - in any bucket, not just Partial - exposes the same logic directly:  for _, voxel in result.Voxels.Extra do voxel:Cleanup(Enums.Cleanup.Fade, 2) end

searchDocs.title: Knowing when it's done​
searchDocs.content: Controller:Shatter(...) fires Enums.Event.Destroyed once every Partial voxel from that call has actually been destroyed (not just had Cleanup started on it):  Wall:Shatter(config):Next(Enums.Event.Destroyed):Then(function(result) print("debris is fully gone") end)   This only fires for Destroy/Fade (anything that calls Instance:Destroy()); Rebuildnever destroys the part, so a call with only Rebuild set never fires Destroyed.

searchDocs.title: Where to go next​
searchDocs.content: Getting started - install Facet and run your first ShatterHitboxes - the four query types and how detection actually worksCleanup - Rebuild, Fade, Destroy, and writing your own  The full API reference lives under the API tab.

searchDocs.title: Install​
searchDocs.content: # wally.toml [dependencies] Facet = "kr3ative/facet@0.1.0"   Or, with no toolchain at all, paste the bootstrap one-liner into the Studio command bar (enable Game Settings → Security → Allow HTTP Requests first):  local h = game:GetService("HttpService") loadstring(h:GetAsync("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mkl48/Facet/master/dist/install.luau"))()   Either way you end up with a Facet module under ReplicatedStorage.

searchDocs.title: Define a controller​
searchDocs.content: local Facet = require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Facet) local Enums = Facet.Enums local Wall = Facet.Define({ Type = Enums.ControllerType.Absolute, VoxelSize = 2, Host = workspace.Wall, })   Host scopes every Shatter call on this controller to workspace.Wall without naming a target each time. Skip it and pass the target explicitly per call instead:Controller:Shatter(workspace.Wall, config).

searchDocs.title: Shatter it​
searchDocs.content: Wall:Shatter({ Hitbox = { Size = Vector3.new(4, 4, 4), CFrame = impactCFrame, HitboxType = Enums.HitboxType.Partial, }, Cleanup = Enums.Cleanup.Fade, }):Next(Enums.Event.NewVoxel):Then(function(result) print(#result.Voxels.Partial, "voxels produced") end)   See DefineConfig andShatterConfig for every option.

searchDocs.title: Next​
searchDocs.content: Head to Hitboxes to see how detection actually picks parts.

searchDocs.title: How detection works​
searchDocs.content: Every candidate part under the target gets indexed into a broad-phase octree built fresh for that one call (src/VoxelController/Octree.luau). The octree narrows candidates down by bounding sphere; an exact geometric test then runs on whatever it returns, picked byQueryType:  QueryType	Exact test	NotesBox (default)	oriented box vs. part overlap	respects the hitbox's CFrame rotation Sphere	distance from CFrame.Position vs. radius	Size is read as a radius Region	axis-aligned bounding box overlap	ignores the hitbox's rotation Raycast	a single workspace:Raycast along ViewVector	one hit only - see Caveats  Because detection runs through the octree instead of workspace:GetPartBoundsInBox, it doesn't depend on the candidate's CanQuery/CanCollide or even on it living in workspace.

searchDocs.title: HitboxType​
searchDocs.content: Partial (the default) slices whatever the hitbox finds into a voxel grid, splitting the result into Partial (inside the hitbox) and Extra (outside it, greedy-meshed). Wholeskips slicing entirely for parts the hitbox fully contains - those come back untouched inResult.Voxels.Whole, each one still carrying a :Shatter() you can call later with no hitbox needed.

searchDocs.title: Caveats​
searchDocs.content: Raycast casts once through the engine and only resolves the single closest hit alongViewVector - there's no multi-hit walk through everything the ray passes through. If you need every part along a ray, run your own workspace:Raycast loop and pass the resulting parts through as a Region/Box hitbox per hit instead.
